_meta.description: tastebench explainer dictionary. One entry per craft feature, brain network, brain ROI group, and edit type. Every compare/optimize line attaches its matching entry; report --llm embeds the whole dictionary.

entry_fields.term: the key, matches a feature/network/edit name
entry_fields.detail: what it measures and why it matters for taste
entry_fields.how_computed: exactly how the number is derived
entry_fields.how_to_act: concrete action (actionable craft + edit only; null otherwise)

sources: SONG_BENCHMARK_THESIS.md (feature taxonomy S3, 12-network labels S4, counterfactual edit templates S5)
Ji et al. 2019, NeuroImage — Cole-Anticevic Brain-wide Network Partition
Berns & Moore 2012; Boksem & Smidts — neuroforecasting: reliability of reward response predicts broad appeal
Salganik et al. 2006, Science — Music Lab: social influence makes hit outcomes irreducibly noisy

time_to_hook.plain: How many seconds pass before the song's first big energy moment lands.
time_to_hook.detail: The most-cited short-form-virality lever: short-form platforms reward songs that pay off fast. A late hook means most listeners leave before the song's best moment, so the taste of references that hook early is dominated by this single number.
time_to_hook.how_computed: First time the smoothed RMS-dB series crosses 85% of its global peak and holds in the top third for ~3 s; falls back to the global RMS-peak time if it never sustains.
time_to_hook.units_range: seconds; pop short-form hits typically 5-15 s, slow builds 25-60 s+
time_to_hook.how_to_act: Cut or compress the intro and arrangement so the first sustained chorus/drop/hook arrives sooner; reference the centroid's time-to-hook as the target.

intro_length.plain: Dead time before anything musically happens.
intro_length.detail: Distinct from time_to_hook: this is how long the track stays below its own 35th-percentile energy. Long intros are taste-defining for cinematic/ambient references and taste-killing for short-form pop references.
intro_length.how_computed: First time the RMS-dB series exceeds the 35th percentile of its own distribution (energy ramp-in point).
intro_length.units_range: seconds; 0-8 s typical for modern pop, longer for build-driven genres
intro_length.how_to_act: Trim silence and low-energy build; start closer to the first musical event the references favor.

chorus_lift_db.plain: How much louder the biggest section is than the quietest.
chorus_lift_db.detail: Proxy for verse->chorus dynamic punch and sectional contrast. A flat dynamic arc reads as 'no payoff'; references with a strong lift are choosing a high-contrast taste. Measured within one track so a rough demo isn't punished for not being mastered.
chorus_lift_db.how_computed: p90 minus p10 of the RMS-dB series (within-track, gain-invariant).
chorus_lift_db.units_range: dB; ~6-20 dB; <6 dB is a flat arc, >20 dB is very dynamic
chorus_lift_db.how_to_act: Pull the verse/pre back (less density, fewer elements, lower level) and/or push the chorus harder so the jump into it is bigger.
chorus_lift_db.refs: SONG_BENCHMARK_THESIS.md S3 (chorus lift / section contrast), S5

loopability.plain: How tonally consistent the song stays from start to finish.
loopability.detail: The literal short-form loop mechanic: a song that returns to the same tonal world loops cleanly in a feed. Low loopability (wandering harmony) breaks the loop and is a strong negative for short-form-leaning taste.
loopability.how_computed: Cosine similarity between the average normalized chroma of the first half and the second half of the track [0..1].
loopability.how_to_act: Commit to one tonal center; reduce key changes and contrasting bridges if the references loop tightly.

tempo.plain: The song's speed in beats per minute.
tempo.detail: Tempo is weak alone but interacts strongly with the rest of the taste signature: references often cluster in a tempo lane, and being far outside that lane changes the entire feel.
tempo.how_computed: librosa global beat-track tempo on the onset envelope.
tempo.how_to_act: If references cluster in a tempo lane and the demo sits outside it, consider re-cutting at or half/double-timing toward that pocket.

tempo_stability.plain: How steady the tempo stays across the track.
tempo_stability.detail: A rock-solid pocket vs. a drifting/rubato feel. References made for movement (dance/short-form) skew to high stability; expressive/acoustic references tolerate lower.
tempo_stability.how_computed: 1 / (1 + tempo_std) where tempo_std is the std of the per-frame tempogram tempo [0..1].
tempo_stability.how_to_act: Tighten timing (grid/quantize or a steadier performance) toward the references' stability if movement matters.

key_stability.plain: How often the song stays in its main key.
key_stability.detail: Fraction of beats whose detected key equals the global key. Frequent modulation hurts loopability and sing-along memorability; references that 'commit to one tonal center' score high here.
key_stability.how_computed: Share of per-beat Krumhansl-key estimates equal to the global key [0..1].
key_stability.how_to_act: Reduce modulations; resolve back to the home key more often, matching the references' tonal commitment.
key_stability.refs: SONG_BENCHMARK_THESIS.md S3, S5 (commit to one tonal center)

brightness.plain: How bright vs. dark the overall sound is.
brightness.detail: Spectral centroid: high = airy/crisp, low = warm/dark. Part song-bones, part production-state; a major axis of timbral taste and one of the strongest catalog-quality levers found in the spec.
brightness.how_computed: Mean spectral centroid in Hz over the track.
brightness.units_range: Hz; corpus cuts dark <1800, neutral 1800-2400, bright >=2400
brightness.how_to_act: Shift the spectral balance toward the references (top-end air / high-shelf, or warmth / low-pass) in the mix or arrangement.

dynamic_range_db.plain: How much the loudness breathes between quiet and loud.
dynamic_range_db.detail: Health of the dynamic arc across the whole song. Over-compressed = no breathing room; references with a healthy range value contrast and let sections land.
dynamic_range_db.how_computed: p95 minus p5 of the full-resolution RMS-dB (sentinel-capped at 100 dB).
dynamic_range_db.units_range: dB; ~4-25 dB; very low = squashed, near 100 = saturation flag
dynamic_range_db.how_to_act: Back off limiting/compression so quiet parts stay quiet; let the dynamic arc match the references.

hook_density_per_min.plain: How often something energetic grabs the listener, per minute.
hook_density_per_min.detail: Rate of distinct high-salience energy events. Too low = nothing to latch onto; too high = no contrast/exhausting. Taste references define the right density.
hook_density_per_min.how_computed: Upcrossings above the 80th-percentile RMS-dB, normalized to track minutes.
hook_density_per_min.how_to_act: Add or remove distinct energetic moments (fills, drops, vocal ad-libs) to match the references' density.

voiced_fraction.plain: How much of the track has a clear sung/voiced pitch.
voiced_fraction.detail: Vocal presence proxy. Descriptive of taste (vocal-forward vs. instrumental references) but not directly prescribed as an edit.
voiced_fraction.how_computed: Fraction of frames with a finite pyin f0 estimate [0..1].

f0_range_octaves.plain: How wide the vocal pitch range is, in octaves.
f0_range_octaves.detail: Melodic ambition of the vocal. Characterizes a reference's vocal taste; reported, not prescribed.
f0_range_octaves.how_computed: log2(p90 / p10) of the voiced f0 distribution.
f0_range_octaves.units_range: octaves; ~0.3-1.5 typical for a lead vocal

flatness.plain: How noise-like vs. tonal the sound is overall.
flatness.detail: Spectral flatness: high = noisy/airy/percussive texture, low = pitched/tonal. A timbral-taste descriptor; reported, not directly prescribed.
flatness.how_computed: Mean spectral flatness over the track.

Auditory.detail: Predicted engagement of the auditory network. High auditory response at the hook is the spec's hypothesis for short-form virality: the sound itself grabs attention.
Auditory.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Auditory ROIs, z-scored across networks; also reported at the energy-hook second and as split-half temporal reliability.
Auditory.units_range: z-score across the 12 networks; reliability in [-1, 1]

Default-Mode.detail: DMN engagement is the spec's hypothesis for durability and reactivation (sleeper potential): songs that recruit memory/self-referential processing keep being replayed.
Default-Mode.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Default-Mode ROIs, z-scored across networks; hook value and reliability also reported.

Frontoparietal.detail: High frontoparietal response = 'cognitive friction'. The spec's hypothesis is that LOW friction predicts launch velocity: an easy-to-process song converts faster on first listen.
Frontoparietal.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Frontoparietal ROIs, z-scored across networks.
Frontoparietal.units_range: z-score; lower is generally better for launch

Cingulo-Opercular.detail: Salience/tonic-alertness network. Moderate engagement = the song commands attention; sustained high engagement can read as effortful.
Cingulo-Opercular.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Cingulo-Opercular ROIs, z-scored.

Dorsal-Attention.detail: Top-down attentional orienting. Relevant to whether arrangement moments successfully steer the listener.
Dorsal-Attention.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Dorsal-Attention ROIs, z-scored.

Language.detail: Language-network engagement reflects how much the words/semantics pull the listener; high for lyric-forward references.
Language.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Language ROIs, z-scored.

Somatomotor.detail: Sensorimotor engagement: the 'makes you move' axis. High for groove/dance references.
Somatomotor.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Somatomotor ROIs, z-scored.

Visual1.detail: Primary-visual engagement. Carries the video/image taste signal in the multimodal path; near-floor for pure audio.
Visual1.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Visual1 ROIs, z-scored.

Visual2.detail: Higher-order visual engagement (scenes, motion). Carries the video taste signal; near-floor for pure audio.
Visual2.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Visual2 ROIs, z-scored.

Posterior-Multimodal.detail: Binds sensory streams; relevant to whether audio+visual cohere in the multimodal path.
Posterior-Multimodal.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Posterior-Multimodal ROIs, z-scored.

Ventral-Multimodal.detail: Higher-order multimodal meaning integration. A small network; descriptive of integrative taste.
Ventral-Multimodal.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Ventral-Multimodal ROIs, z-scored.

Orbito-Affective.detail: Orbitofrontal-affective network: the closest cortical proxy for reward/pleasure. With Default-Mode it forms the reward proxy the spec ties to repeat-worthiness and short-form virality.
Orbito-Affective.how_computed: Mean predicted response over the Cole-Anticevic Orbito-Affective ROIs, z-scored.
Orbito-Affective.refs: SONG_BENCHMARK_THESIS.md S4 (reward + auditory at the hook); Ji et al. 2019

reward_proxy.detail: There is no single cortical 'reward' network in the CAB-NP, so the spec reads repeat-worthiness off the Orbito-Affective + Default-Mode pair. High reward proxy at the hook is the headline short-form-virality hypothesis. Per the neuroforecasting literature it is the *reliability* of this response, not its raw level, that predicts broad appeal.
reward_proxy.how_computed: Mean of the Orbito-Affective and Default-Mode network means (pre-z raw scale).
reward_proxy.refs: SONG_BENCHMARK_THESIS.md S4; Berns & Moore 2012; Boksem & Smidts

hook_reward.plain: How strongly the reward proxy fires at the song's biggest moment.
hook_reward.detail: The reward-proxy value sampled at the energy-hook second specifically. The spec's strongest short-form-virality hypothesis: reward + auditory engagement *at the hook*.
hook_reward.how_computed: Reward-proxy networks' response at the global argmax second of the across-network mean trajectory.
hook_reward.refs: SONG_BENCHMARK_THESIS.md S4, S5 (locate the segment where predicted reward dips)

temporal_reliability.plain: How consistently a network responds across the track.
temporal_reliability.detail: Split-half (even/odd second) correlation of a network's time series. The neuroforecasting result: reliable reward response, not peak level, is what predicts broad appeal.
temporal_reliability.how_computed: Pearson r between even-indexed and odd-indexed seconds of the network's mean time series.
temporal_reliability.refs: Berns & Moore 2012; Boksem & Smidts; DEMO_ANALYSIS_SPEC.md (neural axis)

edit.shorten_intro.plain: Cut the intro so the hook lands sooner.
edit.shorten_intro.detail: Section-level, high-confidence edit. The spec's flagship example: 'Intro 22 s -> 10 s => short-form virality +~18%'.
edit.shorten_intro.how_computed: Counterfactual: lower intro_length / time_to_hook toward the taste centroid and re-score the craft distance; predicted delta is the modeled distance reduction.
edit.shorten_intro.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; reported with a confidence label
edit.shorten_intro.how_to_act: Trim the intro and front-load the first chorus/drop/hook.

edit.raise_chorus_lift.plain: Make the chorus hit harder relative to the verse.
edit.raise_chorus_lift.detail: Section-level, high-confidence edit. Spec: 'Chorus lift is bottom-20% => +4 dB verse->chorus contrast'.
edit.raise_chorus_lift.how_computed: Counterfactual: raise chorus_lift_db toward the taste centroid and re-score.
edit.raise_chorus_lift.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.raise_chorus_lift.how_to_act: Pull the verse/pre back and/or push the chorus so the jump is bigger.

edit.commit_tonal_center.plain: Commit to one key so the song loops cleanly.
edit.commit_tonal_center.detail: Section-level edit. Spec: 'Key modulates 3x (low loopability) => commit to one tonal center for the loop.'
edit.commit_tonal_center.how_computed: Counterfactual: raise key_stability / loopability toward the centroid and re-score.
edit.commit_tonal_center.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.commit_tonal_center.how_to_act: Reduce modulations; resolve back to the home key.

edit.shift_tempo_toward_lane.plain: Move the tempo toward the lane the references cluster in.
edit.shift_tempo_toward_lane.detail: Section-level edit. Spec: 'Tempo 138; hits in your lane cluster 100-110 => consider a slower pocket.'
edit.shift_tempo_toward_lane.how_computed: Counterfactual: move tempo toward the taste centroid (clamped to a musically valid step) and re-score.
edit.shift_tempo_toward_lane.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.shift_tempo_toward_lane.how_to_act: Re-cut at, or half/double-time toward, the references' tempo pocket.

edit.tighten_tempo.plain: Steady the tempo toward the references' pocket consistency.
edit.tighten_tempo.detail: Section-level edit when the references are metronomic and the demo drifts.
edit.tighten_tempo.how_computed: Counterfactual: raise tempo_stability toward the centroid and re-score.
edit.tighten_tempo.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.tighten_tempo.how_to_act: Quantize/grid or perform to a steadier click.

edit.match_brightness.plain: Shift the spectral balance toward the references.
edit.match_brightness.detail: Mix/arrangement edit. Brightness is part production-state, so this is tagged medium-confidence (a demo isn't punished for not being mastered).
edit.match_brightness.how_computed: Counterfactual: move brightness toward the taste centroid and re-score.
edit.match_brightness.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.match_brightness.how_to_act: High-shelf for air or low-pass/warmth toward the references' tone.

edit.open_dynamics.plain: Let the loudness breathe more, toward the references.
edit.open_dynamics.detail: Production edit. Tagged medium-confidence since dynamic range is production-state.
edit.open_dynamics.how_computed: Counterfactual: move dynamic_range_db toward the centroid and re-score.
edit.open_dynamics.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.open_dynamics.how_to_act: Back off limiting/compression so quiet sections stay quiet.

edit.adjust_hook_density.plain: Add or remove energetic moments toward the references' rate.
edit.adjust_hook_density.detail: Arrangement edit: too few moments = nothing to latch onto; too many = no contrast.
edit.adjust_hook_density.how_computed: Counterfactual: move hook_density_per_min toward the centroid and re-score.
edit.adjust_hook_density.units_range: predicted change in taste distance; confidence-labeled
edit.adjust_hook_density.how_to_act: Add/remove fills, drops, ad-libs, or section changes.

brightness_mean.plain: how bright the frame is overall
brightness_mean.detail: Overall luminance — dark/moody vs bright/airy is one of the first things the eye reads and a strong taste signal.
brightness_mean.how_computed: Mean HSV value channel (video: averaged over sampled frames).
brightness_mean.how_to_act: Lift or crush exposure toward the references' brightness band.

contrast.plain: how much light-to-dark separation there is
contrast.detail: Tonal contrast — flat/hazy vs punchy; the visual analogue of audio dynamic range.
contrast.how_computed: Stdev of Rec.709 luminance over the frame.
contrast.how_to_act: Add or pull back an S-curve toward the references.

saturation_mean.plain: how vivid vs muted the colour is
saturation_mean.detail: Pastel/desaturated vs saturated — a core aesthetic axis references share.
saturation_mean.how_to_act: Push/pull global saturation toward the references.

saturation_std.plain: how evenly saturated the frame is
saturation_std.detail: Uniform colour wash vs vivid-pop-on-muted-ground; a texture cue.

colorfulness.detail: Greyscale-ish vs richly chromatic in one number.
colorfulness.how_to_act: Grade toward/away from the references' colour richness.

palette_size.plain: how many dominant colours it uses
palette_size.detail: Tight 2-3 colour scheme vs busy many-colour frame; an immediately legible signature.
palette_size.how_computed: Adaptive median-cut to 16, count clusters >5% of pixels.
palette_size.how_to_act: Lock to a scheme / expand the palette toward the references.

warm_cool_balance.plain: whether colour leans warm or cool
warm_cool_balance.detail: Teal-orange vs cold-blue grade; a defining look.
warm_cool_balance.how_computed: Saturation-weighted warm-hue minus cool-hue pixel fraction.
warm_cool_balance.how_to_act: Shift colour grade / WB toward the references' temperature.

hue_spread.detail: Monochrome/duotone vs full-spectrum; distinct from saturation.
hue_spread.how_computed: Circular stdev of hue over saturated pixels, normalized.
hue_spread.how_to_act: Tighten to a duotone or open the hue range toward the references.

edge_density.plain: how busy / detailed the frame is
edge_density.how_computed: Fraction of pixels with luminance-gradient magnitude over threshold.
edge_density.how_to_act: Simplify (negative space) or add detail toward the references.

center_bias.how_computed: Central-third edge energy / total edge energy.

aspect_ratio.detail: Square/vertical/cinematic-wide format is part of the look. Descriptive.

motion_energy.detail: Static/locked vs kinetic; pacing felt before anything is read.
motion_energy.how_computed: Mean absolute luminance diff between consecutive sampled frames.
motion_energy.how_to_act: Add or calm motion toward the references' energy.

cut_rate_per_min.detail: Long takes vs rapid montage; a strong editorial signal.
cut_rate_per_min.how_computed: Per-frame RGB-histogram L1 spikes over adaptive threshold, per minute.
cut_rate_per_min.how_to_act: Cut more/less often toward the references' pace.

avg_shot_len_s.detail: Seconds the eye rests per shot; inverse of cut rate.
avg_shot_len_s.how_to_act: Lengthen/shorten average shots toward the references.

brightness_drift.detail: Whether it visually evolves vs holds one look. Descriptive arc.
brightness_drift.how_computed: Stdev of per-frame mean brightness across frames.
